new_classes.label_en: Semi-sparkling wine in containers > 2 L and ≤ 10 L (subheading 2204 22 10)
new_classes.label_de: Perlwein in Behältnissen von mehr als 2 L bis einschließlich 10 L (Unterposition 2204 22 10)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes (other than sparkling wine of subheading 2204 10), presented in bottles with mushroom stoppers held in place by ties or fastenings, or otherwise put up with an excess pressure due to dissolved carbon dioxide of not less than 1 bar but less than 3 bar measured at 20 °C, in containers holding more than 2 litres and not more than 10 litres.

coverage_explanation: The principal distinguishing criteria are covered: (1) semi-sparkling character defined by CO₂ excess pressure ≥ 1 bar and < 3 bar at 20 °C — captured via the existing SemiSparklingPressureQuality quality class and the excessPressureBar datatype range restrictions; (2) mushroom stopper presentation — captured via MushroomStopperBottleQuality; (3) container volume range > 2 L and ≤ 10 L — captured via LargeContainer part-of restriction and liquidVolumeInLitres datatype range; (4) exclusion of heading 2204 10 sparkling wines — captured via complement restrictions against Champagne, Prosecco, OtherSparklingWine, and OtherQualitySparklingWine sibling classes; (5) sibling discrimination against the ≤ 2 L semi-sparkling node (22042106to09) via complement. Coverage is not 1.0 because: (a) the LargeContainer TBox class is defined as ≥ 2 L without an upper bound of 10 L, so the upper-bound constraint is expressed only via the decimalRange restriction on liquidVolumeInLitres rather than a single clean equivalence; (b) the legal text references a broader explanatory note to subheadings 22042106 onward which cannot be fully formalised from the text provided; (c) the alternative presentation criterion (bottles with mushroom stoppers OR other containers with ≥ 1 bar) is modelled as a disjunction only partially — both qualities are listed as someValuesFrom rather than an explicit OWL union, which slightly over-restricts in DL semantics.
